<insights>

- High cohesion (0.86) — files are tightly interconnected
- The request-dedup.ts being flagged as the zone entry point while living deep in viewer/messaging suggests this module is the primary consumer-facing interface for message flow control, not a leaf utility — its placement in this scaffolding zone rather than web-viewer may indicate it bridges the two communities.
- At 86% cohesion and 14% coupling, this zone is well-bounded but not perfectly isolated; the coupling likely represents the web-viewer zone importing node-culler.ts and request-dedup.ts, which are shared utilities consumed by the larger viewer community.
- build.js and dev.js as config archetypes alongside viewer component files in the same zone suggests the community detection is grouping together files that are all lightly connected to the broader web-viewer graph rather than forming a strong internal cluster.
- The web zone receives imports from web-viewer (2 imports) and web-integration (1 import), confirming that node-culler and request-dedup are shared infrastructure consumed upstream — consider whether these belong in a dedicated shared-utilities zone to make the dependency direction explicit.
- BROWSER_ERROR_CODE_5.md and MEMORY_PROFILE.md co-located with build config and viewer utilities suggests incident post-mortems are stored ad-hoc alongside source — a dedicated docs/ or .notes/ directory within the package would prevent documentation from polluting the source zone.
- Zone "web-package-scaffolding" has files across 5 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- web-package-scaffolding is the only zone that receives inbound imports from two distinct zones simultaneously (web-viewer: 2, web-integration: 1) while itself having no outbound cross-zone imports — it functions as a pure shared-utilities provider, consumed by both the view layer and the integration layer without depending on either.
- The shared utilities in this zone (node-culler.ts, request-dedup.ts) are consumed independently by web-viewer and web-integration without those two zones forming a direct dependency on each other for those utilities — this fan-out pattern is healthy but means changes to either utility affect two consumers simultaneously.
- web-package-scaffolding acts as an implicit shared-utilities layer consumed by both web-viewer and web-integration, but it has no formal 'shared' or 'common' designation in the package structure — consider promoting these utilities to an explicit shared zone or internal subpackage to make the fan-out contract visible.
- build.js and dev.js (build-tooling config archetypes) are grouped in the same zone as runtime viewer utilities (node-culler.ts, request-dedup.ts) — these serve fundamentally different lifecycles (build time vs. runtime) and their co-location in one algorithmic zone masks the fact that the runtime utilities have implicit consumers while the build scripts do not.
- request-dedup.ts is identified as the zone entry point despite being buried at packages/web/src/viewer/messaging/ — this means the community detection algorithm found it to be the highest-connectivity node in the zone, signaling it has organically become a de facto public contract point without a formal interface declaration. No explicit exports file or interface type formalizes this contract.
- Build tooling (build.js, dev.js) and runtime viewer utilities (node-culler.ts, request-dedup.ts) share a zone boundary with no structural separation. Changes to build configuration could be conflated with changes to the shared runtime surface during code review, and the zone provides no signal about which files are safe to modify in isolation.
- request-dedup.ts is the zone's highest-connectivity node and is consumed by two independent zones (web-viewer, web-integration), but it has no formal public interface or exports contract — it is a de facto shared API discovered algorithmically rather than designed deliberately. This missing abstraction means its signature can be changed without any tooling warning that two consumers exist.
- The two shared utilities use inconsistent naming conventions: node-culler.ts uses the domain-specific suffix -culler while request-dedup.ts uses an abbreviation -dedup — there is no signal from the names alone that these are peer shared-infrastructure utilities rather than unrelated leaf files.
- This zone spans five directories from the package root down to packages/web/src/viewer/messaging/ — any file added to an intermediate directory may inadvertently join or fragment the zone boundary without the author realising it, since zone assignment is algorithmic rather than path-convention-based.
- Normalize the naming convention for shared utilities: either expand abbreviations (request-deduplication.ts) or abbreviate consistently (node-culler.ts → node-cull.ts) so peer utilities are recognisable as a cohesive set at a glance.
- Consolidate node-culler.ts and request-dedup.ts into a formal packages/web/src/shared/ directory with an index.ts barrel — this removes algorithmic zone fragility, makes the shared surface explicit, and prevents future utilities from being scattered across viewer subdirectories.
- [call graph] 131 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 10 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
